message:
feat(stdlib): add freezero + recallocarray (bd-xl4sb)

OpenBSD-origin secret-zeroing allocator primitives, now adopted by
glibc 2.34+. Both extend our existing reallocarray to defeat use-
after-free leaks of credentials, session tokens, and other secret-
bearing buffers.

* freezero(ptr, size) — explicit_bzero(size bytes at ptr) then free.
  NULL is a no-op (matches free(NULL)).
* recallocarray(ptr, oldnmemb, nmemb, size) — overflow-checked
  reallocarray that zeros the freshly-acquired tail on grow and
  zeros the released tail on shrink. NULL+oldnmemb=0 acts as
  calloc; new_size=0 acts as free with secret-zeroing; mismatched
  oldnmemb sets EINVAL.

Both shims live in stdlib_abi.rs alongside reallocarray and
delegate to existing malloc_abi::{calloc,realloc,free} +
string_abi::explicit_bzero — no new core module needed since the
work is pointer-arithmetic + libc-malloc orchestration.

9 integration tests covering NULL safety, calloc-equivalent path,
overflow → ENOMEM, mismatched oldnmemb → EINVAL, grow zeros tail,
shrink preserves prefix, zero-size frees and returns NULL. Clippy-
clean, fmt-clean.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
